channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rep. Burchett: Congress to blame if Trump agenda fails
item.description: GOP Tennessee Rep. Tim Burchett tells NewsNation he knows whose fault it will be if Donald Trump's agenda doesn't succeed.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE AND REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of homicide in shooting death of man in crisis
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy was convicted of criminally negligent homicide Thursday in the shooting death of a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help after his car got stuck in a small mountain community.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate ready to stay up all night to pass GOP budget over objections from Democrats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senators are ready to stay up all night, having launched a budget “vote-a-rama” late Thursday in a crucial, if dreaded, step toward unleashing a $340 billion package President Donald Trump's team says it needs for mass deportations and security measures that top the Republican agen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The man accused of stabbing Salman Rushdie declines to take the stand as the defense rests
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The New Jersey man on trial in the 2022 stabbing of author Salman Rushdie declined to testify in his defense Thursday as his lawyers rested their case without calling any witnesses.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe, an 8-year NFL veteran who played his entire career with the Minnesota Vikings, took to the podium to voice his opposition to the plaque design.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: CEO confidence hits highest point in 3 years: Survey
item.description: Forty-four percent in The Conference Board survey said economic conditions have become more preferable “than six months ago.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Some New York prison guards charged in beating death of handcuffed inmate appear in court
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Six New York prison guards have been indicted for second-degree murder in the beating death of a handcuffed inmate, a brutal incident captured on body-worn cameras that triggered widespread outrage and calls for justice.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell announced Thursday that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Inmates takeover another New York prison, all visits canceled day after Gov. Hochul deploys National Guard
item.description: Dozens of correctional facilities canceled prison visits one day after New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order to provide National Guard members to provide extra security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lifetime, Chili’s celebrate National Margarita Day with new rom-com feature
item.description: Love, laughter, and lime wedges take center stage in Lifetime’s latest feel-good romance, created in partnership with Chili’s Bar & Grill, to celebrate National Margarita Day, which is coming up on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: USS Harry S. Truman commanding officer relieved after collision with merchant ship near Suez Canal
item.description: The commanding officer of the U.S. Navy aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman was relieved of his duty on Thursday, just over a week after the vessel collided with a merchant ship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, KRON4

item.title: Snowy roads lead to hundreds of Virginia and North Carolina crashes as Arctic air brings record cold
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Roads in Virginia and North Carolina remained treacherous Thursday as unusually heavy snow led to hundreds of accidents, including crashes on an interstate that involved more than 50 vehicles.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, MAKIYA SEMINERA and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taco Bell, Milk Bar team up again for a sweet new treat
item.description: Taco Bell and Milk Bar are teaming up once again for a new sweet treat. The brands announced the launch of the new Milk Bar Birthday Cake Churros, a little treat filled with birthday cake frosting and topped with bright pink confetti sprinkles.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Lt. Gov. says Texas Lottery will end if mobile lottery apps aren't banned
item.description: "It is a hard line, couriers have to go," Texas Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick said on Wednesday. The statement comes on the heels of an $83.5 million Lotto Texas jackpot, the fifth-highest in the game's history, which was won through the app Jackpocket -- a DraftKings subsidiary.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Peck, NewsNation

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, WGN 9

item.title: Black and blue, or white and gold? 'The dress' turns 10 years old
item.description: Ten years ago today, a debate started when a dress was posted on the internet asking what color people were seeing.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies. State and local health departments are only just beginning to hear from officials at the CDC, nearly a month after the inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coast Guard intercepts 'panga' boat with 20 suspected illegal migrants in waters off San Diego
item.description: Twenty migrants were caught by a U.S. Coast Guard crew that interdicted their boat off the waters of San Diego on Tuesday night, the military branch said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Postmaster general to step down, asks for replacement
item.description: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy, a former prominent Trump fundraiser who was appointed during the president's first term, announced Tuesday he would be stepping down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: How better water systems can help a city survive the next firestorm
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Colorado (AP) — Santa Rosa. Paradise. Boulder County. Lahaina. Los Angeles. All are places that have shown that American cities and their water systems weren't built to withstand wildfire, experts say. Hydrants trickled. Pumps and treatment plants lost power.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta jet flips upside down on a snowy Toronto runway and all 80 aboard survive
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A Delta Air Lines jet flipped on its roof while landing Monday at Toronto’s Pearson Airport, but all 80 people on board survived and those hurt had relatively minor injuries, the airport’s chief executive said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE access of recipient information: AP sources
item.description: The Social Security Administration's acting commissioner has stepped down from her role at the agency over Department of Government Efficiency requests to access Social Security recipient information, according to two people familiar with the official's departure who were not authorized to discuss t
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southwest Airlines is slashing 15% of its corporate workforce, its first major layoffs in 53 years
item.description: Southwest Airlines is eliminating 1,750 jobs, or 15% of its corporate workforce, in the first major layoffs in the company's 53-year history.
item.dc:creator: ALEX VEIGA, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, ABC 4
